data.description:
## Why

Flipping `identity.require_authenticated` with a stale/wrong `enforce_since` would fail the
merge-gate on legitimate **historical** unsigned events. The boundary must be pinned to the
enforcement-cutover commit and **proven** to grandfather everything before it, so the flip is
safe. This is the remediation of the requirement check the rollout depends on.

## What

Verify and repin the grandfathering boundary, with a regression test around it:

- In `src/rebar/_commands/verify_authorship.py` and `src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, verify the
  `enforce_since` grandfathering logic (pre-boundary events are exempt; post-boundary in-scope
  events are enforced).
- Repin `identity.enforce_since` in `rebar.toml` to the enforcement-cutover commit, with a
  documented rationale for the chosen commit.
- Add a regression test asserting the boundary behaviour in both directions.

## Scope

The grandfathering boundary check + config + a regression test. NOT the enforcement flip
itself (that is the atomic-flip story).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `rebar verify-identity` over the full history exits 0 with **zero** pre-`enforce_since`
      events flagged.
- [ ] A regression test asserts: a synthetic **unsigned** event before the boundary PASSES,
      and a new unsigned/forged in-scope event after the boundary FAILS. Proving command:
      `make test` (the new test).
- [ ] `identity.enforce_since` in `rebar.toml` is pinned to the enforcement-cutover commit,
      with the rationale for that commit documented in the change.
data.title: Verify and repin identity.enforce_since so pre-flip events are grandfathered
